examples.title: Basic Runtime Polymorphism with Virtual Function
examples.description: Demonstrates how base class pointer can call derived class methods at runtime using virtual functions
examples.explanation:
1. The 'virtual' keyword tells compiler to use dynamic dispatch
2. Base pointer 'animal1' holds address of Dog object
3. When sound() is called, C++ checks actual object type at runtime
4. Dog's sound() is invoked (not Animal's)
5. Virtual destructor ensures proper derived class destructor is called

examples.title: Array of Base Class Pointers with Polymorphism
examples.description: Shows how to store multiple derived class objects in an array of base class pointers and process them uniformly
examples.explanation:
1. Shape is an abstract class with pure virtual function (area() = 0)
2. Cannot create objects of abstract class, only pointers
3. Rectangle, Circle, Triangle override area() and display()
4. shapes[] is an array of Shape pointers storing different derived objects
5. Loop iterates through each pointer, calling display() - correct version invoked at runtime
6. This demonstrates the power of runtime polymorphism for processing heterogeneous objects uniformly
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of shapes
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing n shape pointers

examples.title: Runtime Polymorphism with Function Parameters and Typeid
examples.description: Advanced example showing runtime polymorphism through function parameters and runtime type identification (RTTI)
examples.explanation:
1. Function processEmployee() accepts Employee* - base class pointer
2. Same function handles Manager, Developer, and Employee objects polymorphically
3. typeid(*emp) provides Runtime Type Identification (RTTI)
4. dynamic_cast<Manager*> safely downcasts the base pointer to derived class
5. Virtual function table (vtable) is used by compiler to resolve function calls at runtime
6. Each derived class has its own vtable entry for calculateBonus() override
7. The correct bonus calculation is invoked based on actual object type at runtime
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of employees processed
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing employee objects and pointers
